title: How to Prepare for Ramadan: The Ultimate Spiritual & Practical Guide
excerpt: Ramadan is the greatest month of the year — but only for those who prepare for it. This guide shows you exactly how to get spiritually, physically, and practically ready so you can maximise every single day of the blessed month.
author: QuranBookk
content:
The Prophet ﷺ would begin preparing for Ramadan two months before its arrival — increasing worship in Rajab and Sha'ban. When Rajab entered, he would say:

<HadithBlock
  text="O Allah, bless us in Rajab and Sha'ban, and allow us to reach Ramadan."
  narrator="Anas ibn Malik (رضي الله عنه)"
  source="Musnad Ahmad — Classified as weak but the dua is widely practised as it does not conflict with Islamic principles"
/>

The scholars say: whoever does not prepare for Ramadan before it arrives, wastes its first week settling in. And Ramadan is too precious to waste a single day, let alone seven.

## Why Preparation Is Non-Negotiable

Ramadan arrives with a window unlike any other month. The Prophet ﷺ described it:

<HadithBlock
  text="When Ramadan comes, the gates of Paradise are opened, the gates of Hell are closed, and the devils are chained."
  narrator="Abu Hurairah (رضي الله عنه)"
  source="Sahih al-Bukhari 1899, Sahih Muslim 1079"
/>

This is the spiritual environment. But environment alone does not produce transformation — habits do. The person who enters Ramadan with established habits of Quran recitation, night prayer, and dhikr will exponentially multiply their output. The person who enters cold and unprepared will spend the first two weeks adjusting.

## Spiritual Preparation

### Step 1: Begin increasing worship in Sha'ban

The Prophet ﷺ fasted more in Sha'ban than any month other than Ramadan. Aisha (رضي الله عنها) said he fasted most of Sha'ban. This serves as a warm-up — your body, willpower, and spiritual routine are already calibrated when Ramadan arrives.

Start with: fasting Mondays and Thursdays (a confirmed sunnah), increasing Quran recitation, and adding a voluntary night prayer even if just two rak'ahs.

### Step 2: Make sincere tawbah (repentance)

Ramadan is the greatest opportunity for forgiveness, but it amplifies whatever state you bring into it. Clear your slate before the month begins. The Prophet ﷺ described as wretched the one who enters and exits Ramadan without having been forgiven.

Make a genuine tawbah: acknowledge the sin, feel remorse, resolve never to return to it, and where applicable, make restitution (returning owed money, seeking forgiveness from those you wronged).

### Step 3: Set a Quran completion goal

The Quran was revealed in Ramadan. Jibril would review the entire Quran with the Prophet ﷺ each Ramadan — and in his final Ramadan, they reviewed it twice.

<AyahBlock
  arabic="شَهْرُ رَمَضَانَ ٱلَّذِىٓ أُنزِلَ فِيهِ ٱلْقُرْءَانُ"
  translation="The month of Ramadan in which was revealed the Quran."
  reference="Surah Al-Baqarah 2:185"
/>

A realistic Quran completion plan for Ramadan: the Quran is 30 juz (parts). Reading 1 juz per day completes the Quran once. Reading 2 juz per day completes it twice. At roughly 20 minutes per juz for a fluent reciter, this is very achievable. Use QuranBookk's [Quran Juzs reader](/quran/juzs) to track your progress through each juz.

### Step 4: Set a tahajjud and Taraweeh plan

Taraweeh (the long night prayers in Ramadan) is the prayer of the Quran being recited in full over the month in traditional mosques following the Malikiyya timetable. Attending is a powerful way to both complete the Quran in listening *and* spend the nights in worship.

The Prophet ﷺ said: *"Whoever stands in prayer in Ramadan out of faith and seeking reward, his previous sins will be forgiven."* (Sahih al-Bukhari 37)

## Physical Preparation

### Adjust your sleep gradually

In Ramadan, the ideal schedule is: sleep after Isha, wake for Tahajjud/Suhoor, pray Fajr, sleep again until work. This requires moving your normal bedtime earlier by 1–2 hours. Start doing this 2 weeks before Ramadan.

### Train your stomach

If you eat less in Sha'ban — smaller portions, fewer snacks — your stomach adjusts its size and the fast becomes significantly easier. Many people find the first 3–5 days of Ramadan physically difficult purely because they had been overeating beforehand.

### Stockpile suhoor-appropriate foods

Good suhoor foods: oats and slow-release carbohydrates, eggs, dates, high-fibre foods, and plenty of water. Avoid salty foods that increase thirst and sugary foods that spike then crash your energy. Prepare your kitchen before Ramadan so the month itself flows without logistical friction.

## Practical Preparation

### Reduce digital distractions now

The biggest waster of Ramadan time for modern Muslims is the smartphone. Social media, YouTube, and entertainment consume the very hours that should be spent in worship. Begin reducing these habits *before* Ramadan so you do not spend the month fighting an addiction at its peak.

### Plan your Zakat and Sadaqah

Charity is multiplied in Ramadan. Calculate your Zakat now (if a year has passed on your savings above the nisab threshold) so you can give it in Ramadan. Plan your daily sadaqah — even a dirham given daily throughout Ramadan is an extraordinary investment.

QuranBookk's [Zakat calculator](/zakat/calculator) helps you work out exactly how much you owe.

### Set specific daily goals

Not goals like "be a better Muslim" — specific, measurable goals. For example:
- Fajr prayer: in masjid every day
- Quran: 2 juz per day
- Taraweeh: attend every night
- Dhikr: 100 subhanAllah after each prayer
- Charity: £1 per day to a chosen cause

## Use the Full Month of Ramadan at QuranBookk

QuranBookk provides a comprehensive Ramadan toolkit: daily prayer times with Suhoor and Iftar times for your location, a Quran completion tracker, guided dhikr collections, Zakat calculator, and Laylat al-Qadr guides for the last ten nights.

👉 [Access QuranBookk's Ramadan resources](/features)

Ramadan is not a vacation from life — it is the most intense month of spiritual work in the year. Prepare accordingly, and it will transform you in ways that last the other eleven months.
